description:
Legal document in the hand of Ḥalfon b. Menashshe ha-Levi (fl. 1100–1138 CE). Involves a woman named Sitt al-Ahl, a pair of bracelets or armlets (al-zawj al-damālij), and a woman named Sutūt. Sitt al-Ahl has received a sum of money from Sutūt and releases her from all claims. Likely an indirect join with the upper right fragment under the shelfmark BL OR 13153.4/1 (PGPID 19170).

On verso, there is a list, also in Ḥalfon's hand mentioning Abū Naṣr b. Surūr; the late Mawhūb; and expenses for the capitation tax and perhaps a funeral.
description_he: מסמך משפטי בכתב ידו של חלפון בן מנשה. סִתּ אל-אהל מקבלת כסף מסוטוט ומשחררת אותה מכל תביעה. בצד ב: רשימת הוצאות לגלגולת וייתכן שלהלוויה.
